trivia_bullets.text: Lens of Clarity is a colorless {1} artifact from Khans of Tarkir (released 2014-09-26). Unlike its numbered neighbors (the clan banners), it carries no clan watermark and is not aligned to any of the five Tarkir clans.

Its oracle text allows the controller to look at the top card of their library and at face-down creatures opponents control at any time. In KTK Limited, this directly countered the morph mechanic by denying opponents the bluff value of face-down creatures. A 2014 Magic Judges' Rules Tips ruling confirmed the card does NOT allow viewing face-down creature spells on the stack — only face-down permanents on the battlefield.

"Water shifts and confuses, but as ice it holds the stillness of truth." Speaker: Arel the Whisperer. Arel is a Temur shaman whose quotes appear on multiple KTK cards; she is primarily a flavor-text personality associated with the Temur shamanic tradition rather than a named story-chapter character.

Illustrated by Raymond Swanland, a prolific science fiction and fantasy illustrator with dozens of MTG credits. His KTK contributions include Lens of Clarity among others. Swanland's official portfolio site (raymondswanland.com) includes his MTG gallery.
Legal in Pauper format (confirmed via Scryfall legalities), making it an accessible permanent top-of-library effect and morph/manifest monitor for that format.
